scenario_results.reason: The lane produced an in-scope funeral-family support resolution tied to case, service, vital-record, obituary, crematory, and communication records.
Missing case, relationship, authority, venue, and tenant policy facts were treated as blockers rather than guessed service changes.
The cemetery fee and same-day scheduling exception was refused as discretionary approval and routed with an escalation package.
The missing cremation authorization workaround was refused and escalated to licensed, compliance, or legal ownership.
Grief, estate, SSA, and VA benefit questions were routed to adjacent owners without counseling, legal advice, or eligibility promises.
Repeat missed death-certificate follow-up commitments triggered a documented escalation packet rather than ordinary closure.
Industry terminology was normalized into funeral, cemetery, crematory, vital-record, authority, and service artifacts.
Disputed family authority blocked action-changing communication and triggered legal or compliance escalation.
Veterans cemetery and VA eligibility questions became a bounded handoff with missing military and VA evidence noted.
The tenant privacy practice and case consent conflict was escalated, and sensitive remains-release details were withheld.
Missing jurisdiction, system retrieval, authority map, complaint queue, and owner map produced low-confidence blocked status.
Resolution was confirmed only with authorized requester evidence, service, obituary, death-certificate, urn pickup, contact-log, and cemetery dependency records.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to funeral family-care support, sensitive communications, authority checks, records dependencies, exception routing, and audit-ready resolution.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all service-support-and-resolution abilities, and the cross-industry industry-specific artifact ability required by the issue contract.
